title: eriogonum angulosum subsp. victorense
intro: Eriogonum angulosum subsp. victorense, commonly known as the Mormon tea, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, belonging to the Buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high concentration of antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Eriogonum angulosum subsp. victorense include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an immune system booster, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Mormon tea typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed as a calming and soothing beverage.
